scenario.name: Review And Revision
scenario.hypothesis: A draft-review-revise task should reveal whether extra participants improve correctness enough to justify overhead.
scenario.expectedDivergence: central or choreography should show explicit critique/revision evidence; single may be faster but more likely to miss edge cases.
scenario.taskPacket:
# Agent Coordination Patterns Task Packet

This packet is self-contained. Do not inspect the repository, shell, or filesystem.
Use only the Firegrid session and coordination-channel tools exposed to you.

Scenario: Review And Revision

Provided materials:

Experiment goal:
Firegrid should act as the workbench for comparing three coordination patterns: one participant, a central orchestrator, and peer choreography.

Current harness shape:
- The driver composes a real Firegrid host in-process.
- Participants are launched through the public Firegrid client/session surface.
- The shared board is exposed as channels: coordination.work, coordination.claims, coordination.findings, coordination.questions, coordination.reviews, and coordination.final.
- The driver injects inbound events through Firegrid.channels.send and waits for the final artifact through Firegrid.channels.waitFor.
- A run is incomplete unless a coordination.final row is published.

Known measurement risks:
- If the task packet asks for repository or shell access, agents may burn time on unavailable tools instead of exercising Firegrid coordination.
- If final artifacts are optional prose instead of channel rows, the scorer cannot reliably compare arms.
- Choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not by hidden driver state.

Task:
- Draft a small improvement plan for the experiment harness.
- Review the plan for failure modes or measurement bias.
- Revise the plan once based on the review.
- The final artifact must separate draft, review findings, and revised plan.

Output contract:
- Produce one concise final artifact with body under 1,200 characters.
- State which evidence you inspected.
- State the implementation or design recommendation.
- State open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why.

finalArtifact.title: Review-and-revision: harness improvement plan (single arm)
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected: only the task packet (no repo/shell access available or used).

DRAFT PLAN:
1. Make coordination.final the sole success oracle; scorer ignores prose.
2. Add board-schema validation at send time (kind, title, status, workId).
3. Emit per-arm trace of channel rows with monotonic seq for replay.
4. Provide a packet-only contract; forbid repo/shell tool calls in arms.
5. For choreography arm, score on durable board state (claims/findings/reviews/final) not driver memory.

REVIEW FINDINGS:
A. Bias: single-arm has no claims/reviews; scoring across arms must not penalize empty boards when the protocol does not require them.
B. Failure mode: schema validation at send hides arm bugs; should warn+record, not drop.
C. Measurement risk: per-arm trace seq may diverge if host clock used; need logical seq from channel itself.
D. Hidden state: 'durable board behavior' still depends on what the driver waitFor matches on; match predicates must be published with the run.
E. Tool-boundary leak: packet says no shell, but agents may still attempt; need explicit deny + observable refusal metric.

REVISED PLAN:
1. Scorer keyed on coordination.final only; per-arm rubric declares which other channels are required (single: none; orchestrator: work+claims+final; choreography: findings+reviews+final).
2. Validate final payload shape on receive; surface schema misses as a scored defect, do not drop.
3. Use channel-assigned sequence for traces; publish driver waitFor predicates alongside the run id.
4. Record tool-boundary violations (shell/fs attempts) as a separate metric, not a hard fail.
5. Keep packet self-contained; include the rubric the scorer will apply.

RECOMMENDATION: Adopt revised plan; land rubric+predicates as part of the run manifest so arms are comparable.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
- Is logical channel seq already monotonic across arms?
- Should refusal-to-use-shell be rewarded or just neutral?
- How is partial progress credited when an arm publishes final but skips intermediate channels its rubric required?

finalArtifact.title: Review-and-Revision: Harness improvement plan (central arm)
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected: task packet + replies from two child sessions (drafter, reviewer) via session.agent_output TextChunks.

DRAFT (from drafter child): plan bullets included contract-enforced coordination.final schema/scorer; agents told only Firegrid tools (no shell/fs); pre-flight self-check rejecting prose-only finals; structured per-arm trace of claims/findings/reviews counts for scoring parity.

REVIEW (from reviewer child): top biases to guard: arm-asymmetric instrumentation; tool-access drift (must hold identical channel/tool/permission surface per arm); prose-vs-row final ambiguity; hidden driver coupling leaking choreography state; scoring leakage via tool-allowlist diffs.

REVISED PLAN: (1) Make coordination.final a typed row with required fields {kind,title,body,status,workId,arm}; reject prose-only. (2) Fix identical tool surface per arm (only Firegrid session+channel tools; no shell/fs/exec). (3) Driver observes ONLY channel rows; assert no out-of-band reads. (4) Per-arm counters (claims/findings/reviews/questions) emitted as a meta row. (5) Pre-flight harness self-check that exercises each channel once before the timed run.

Recommendation: ship (1)+(2)+(3) as the minimum viable parity fix; (4)+(5) follow.

Open questions: how to score choreography fairness when peers stay silent; whether to bound child-session count per arm; how to detect a participant that fabricates rows it never acted on.

Coordination effect: delegation produced both a draft and an independent bias checklist before revision; the reviewer's tool-access-drift point reshaped bullet (2) from 'document tools' to 'enforce identical surface'.

finalArtifact.title: Final: Experiment harness improvement plan (review-revision)
finalArtifact.body:
DRAFT: Board-only oracle; driver schema-validates; require claims+(findings|reviews) before final; <=1200-char final; arm-agnostic probe each peer >=1 non-final row; synth questions row for missing tools.

REVIEW: F1 oracle needs deterministic fold. F2 driver-as-judge contaminates arm; schema must be peer-consumed contract. F3 defer-vs-discard unspecified. F4 driver speaking for peers contaminates choreography. B1 board createdAt only. B2 uniqueness metric. B3 arm-blind scoring. B4 truncation = board row. E1 final tiebreak; E2 cycle bound; E3 claim TTL; E4 empty-board timeout; E5 per-runId scoping; E6 per-section budgets.

REVISED: R1 fold by (createdAt,rowId), dedup keep-last. R2 schemaVersion on rows; peers self-reject; driver never judges. R3 defer not discard; latency=acceptedAt-firstWork. R4 truncation receipt = findings row. R5 probe scored unlabeled. R6 driver only publishes capability manifest. R7 board timestamps only. R8 unique/total per channel. R9 final tiebreak min createdAt then rowId. R10 cycle cap=3 stalemate. R11 claim TTL=120s. R12 empty-board 180s timeout. R13 channel-layer runId scoping. R14 sections <=350/350/400. R15 only structured finals=complete. Coord helped: reviewer F2/F4 reframed R2/R6; E1-E6 produced R9-R14 absent from draft.
